instruction: Design relational and document data models that are correct, normalized, and performant. Use to create entity-relationship diagrams, decide normalization level, design junction tables, choose data types, and balance read vs write performance. Base operacional: # Playbook  ## Objective Design correct, performant, and evolvable data models that accurately represent the domain.  ## Rules of Entity Design

workflow.action: Identify entities, attributes, and relationships from domain requirements.
Create ERD — define cardinality (1:1, 1:N, M:N) and mandatory vs optional relationships.
Normalize to 3NF — eliminate redundancy while preserving query performance.
Evaluate denormalization — add computed columns or materialized views where read performance justifies.
Choose data types — prefer specific types (uuid, timestamptz, numeric) over generic (text, json).
Document model with table definitions, constraints, indexes, and relationship rationale.

outputContract: ERD diagram with entities and relationships.
Table definitions with columns, types, and constraints.
